passages.title: わたしの一日
passages.en: I get up at seven every morning. I eat breakfast and leave home at eight. I go to school by train. I study Japanese from nine to three. I eat lunch with my friends at school. I return home at four. At night I watch TV and go to bed at eleven.

questions.q: What time does the writer get up?
How does the writer go to school?
What does the writer do at night?

passages.title: わたしの家族
body: はとてもおいしいです。
passages.en: There are four people in my family: my father, my mother, my older sister, and me. My father is a company employee, and he goes to his company by car every day. My mother is a school teacher. Her school is near our house. My older sister is a university student and is studying English. Her cooking is very delicious. On Sundays, our family often goes to the park.

questions.q: How many people are in the writer's family?
How does the father go to work?
What does the family often do on Sundays?

passages.title: 買い物
passages.en: Yesterday, I went shopping at a department store with my mother. My mother bought a new bag. It was 3,000 yen. I wanted to buy a white shirt, but it was expensive, so I didn't buy it. After that, the two of us drank coffee at a shop. It was a very fun day.

questions.q: Who did the writer go shopping with?
Why didn't the writer buy the shirt?

passages.title: 今日の天気
body: たちがあそんでいました。
passages.en: The weather has been nice since this morning. The sky is blue, and there is no wind. I took a walk in the park in the morning. There were many people in the park. Children were playing. But according to the weather forecast, it will rain tomorrow. So tomorrow I will read books at home.

questions.q: What is the weather like today?
Where did the writer take a walk?
What will the writer do tomorrow?

passages.title: 友達への手紙
body: はむずかしいですが、とてもおもしろいです。
おへんじをまっています。
passages.en: Dear Yuki, how are you? I am fine. Last month, I came to Japan. Now I live in Tokyo. I study every day at a Japanese language school. Japanese is difficult, but it is very interesting. Next month, I will go to Kyoto with my friends. Won't you come with us? I am waiting for your reply.

questions.q: Where does the writer live now?
What does the writer say about Japanese?
What does the writer invite Yuki to do?
